growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: fierer.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: LMO-812 (with 5 mM N-acetyl-D-glucosamine as sole carbon source)

up.method: model-predicted exchange flux only; no experimental uptake rate measured

sec.method: model-predicted (produced via acetate kinase ACKr in 15C and 20C models); not experimentally measured

growth_records.cite: Dufault-Thompson, Keith et al. Reconstruction and Analysis of Thermodynamically Constrained Models Reveal Metabolic Responses of a Deep-Sea Bacterium to Temperature Perturbations. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00588-22. [PMC9426432]
growth_records.snippet: Each simulation was performed by constraining the biomass flux to a random value selected within the range of experimentally determined growth rates (0.110 and 0.112 for 4C, 0.254 and 0.255 for 15C, and 0.042 and 0.135 for 20C).
growth_records.notes: Experimental specific growth rate measured for WP2 at 4C = range 0.110-0.112 h^-1 (average over 3 biological replicates), calculated from OD600 slope during exponential phase (OD600 converted to gDW via established Shewanella OD-dryweight relationship). Midpoint mu=(0.110+0.112)/2=0.111 h^-1. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.111=6.24 h. Units h^-1 inferred (turbidity monitored over hours; growth rates from exponential phase). This is a thermodynamically constrained GEM paper; uptake/secretion rates are model-predicted exchange fluxes (constrained to LMO-812 media, biomass fixed to experimental mu), NOT experimentally measured, so only mu is experimental. pH not stated for culture (model pH constrained 6-8). Full LMO-812 composition given by reference (cited ref 33), not reproduced in text.

growth_records.medium: LMO-812 (with 5 mM N-acetyl-D-glucosamine as sole carbon source)

up.method: model-predicted exchange flux only; no experimental uptake rate measured

sec.method: model-predicted (produced via acetate kinase ACKr in 15C model); not experimentally measured

growth_records.cite: Dufault-Thompson, Keith et al. Reconstruction and Analysis of Thermodynamically Constrained Models Reveal Metabolic Responses of a Deep-Sea Bacterium to Temperature Perturbations. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00588-22. [PMC9426432]
growth_records.snippet: constraining the biomass flux to a random value selected within the range of experimentally determined growth rates (0.110 and 0.112 for 4C, 0.254 and 0.255 for 15C, and 0.042 and 0.135 for 20C). ... the 15C model showed the highest metabol
growth_records.notes: Experimental specific growth rate for WP2 at optimal 15C = range 0.254-0.255 h^-1 (average over 3 biological replicates), from OD600 exponential-phase slope. Midpoint mu=(0.254+0.255)/2=0.2545 h^-1. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.2545=2.72 h. Highest growth rate of the three temperatures (optimal). Uptake/secretion rates are model-predicted exchange fluxes only, not experimentally measured. Full LMO-812 composition by reference (ref 33), not in text.

growth_records.medium: LMO-812 (with 5 mM N-acetyl-D-glucosamine as sole carbon source)

up.method: model-predicted exchange flux only; no experimental uptake rate measured

sec.method: model-predicted (produced via acetate kinase ACKr in 20C model); not experimentally measured

growth_records.cite: Dufault-Thompson, Keith et al. Reconstruction and Analysis of Thermodynamically Constrained Models Reveal Metabolic Responses of a Deep-Sea Bacterium to Temperature Perturbations. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00588-22. [PMC9426432]
growth_records.snippet: experimentally determined growth rates (... 0.042 and 0.135 for 20C). ... 20C revealed an earlier start of the exponential growth phase but a lower average growth rate than the suboptimal temperature of 4C ... 20C represents the highest tem
growth_records.notes: Experimental specific growth rate for WP2 at supraoptimal 20C = wide range 0.042-0.135 h^-1 (large spread reflects high replicate variability under high-temperature stress). Midpoint mu=(0.042+0.135)/2=0.0885 h^-1. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.0885=7.83 h. Confidence medium because the reported range is broad (growth deficiency / stress at 20C). Lower average growth than 4C despite earlier exponential onset. Uptake/secretion rates model-predicted only, not experimental. Full LMO-812 composition by reference (ref 33).

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 134206 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/134206
